title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink accepts malformed password environment names
summary: The Oracle MySQL sink did not validate the shape of password_env before runtime secret lookup.
observed: A malformed environment variable name containing spaces could be accepted by configuration validation.
expected: password_env must use a strict environment-variable name format.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for password_env validation.
impact: Misconfigured secret references are harder to diagnose and can hide deployment mistakes.
delivery_semantics: The fix is startup-only validation and does not change message write behavior.
security: Strict secret reference names reduce accidental secret confusion and public configuration mistakes.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL configuration rejects malformed password_env names.
Uppercase names using letters, numbers, and underscores remain accepted.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL unit test suite.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the validation fix is published.
